commands.repair_reason: - [P2] No narrow automated repair is needed; maintainers need to accept the all-agent default/security boundary and coordinate duplicate PRs.; Needs attention: No supply-chain issue was found, but the diff intentionally changes a cross-agent data visibility boundary that maintainers should accept before merge. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds explicit Usage agent scoping so the Control UI requests all configured agents by default, selected agents use `agentId`, gateway cost/session usage can honor those scopes, and protocol docs/generated Swift/tests are updated.

PR surface: Source +277, Tests +484, Docs +2, Other +4. Total +767 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main's Usage controller sends no agent scope, while current main's `sessions.usage` handler defaults omitted `agentId` to the configured default agent. A live multi-agent browser repro was not run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No narrow automated repair is needed; maintainers need to accept the all-agent default/security boundary and coordinate duplicate PRs.
